text:
Market today: Mixed signals for tech/chips. AI sentiment gets a boost from Cramer and cybersecurity spending news, but IBM's profit warning suggests caution in hardware. Your book is right in the middle of these crosscurrents.

Your holdings: SOXL (-30.59%) and SPAL (-36.78%) are flashing red. Both are leveraged ETFs, and on margin, these drawdowns are amplified and need immediate attention. Your other core tech/chip holdings are holding up better.

Watch: Keep a very close eye on your leveraged positions (SOXL, SPAL) and their stops. Also, watch for follow-through in cybersecurity names given today's rally.

Bottom line: Risk management on your leveraged positions is paramount, especially with conflicting signals in the broader tech sector.
plays: SOXL: Consider trimming or exiting SOXL at its current price of 176.66, well below its default stop of 200, given its SELL status and significant -30.59% loss.
SPAL: Review SPAL, currently at 15.90 with a -36.78% loss, against its default stop of 21.38. This is a new, leveraged position that's moving fast against you.
ZS: If you want to add to cybersecurity, ZS is on the scanner watchlist with an 8/12 score and a "price in daily discount" reason. Entry 151.94-151.79, stop 151.56.
